title: Why do I have to book travel in the program?
h1: Why do I have to book travel in the program?
markdown:
# **Why do I have to book travel in the program?**

Booking within [Amazon Travel & Events](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/) is required. The program provides safety, cost savings, booking simplicity, sustainability, and service quality benefits that are not available on consumer booking sites.

## **Benefits of booking within Amazon Travel & Events**

- Safety and security: Amazon Travel & Events provide 24/7 emergency support with instant access to your travel details for quick assistance. All bookings are automatically registered for security monitoring and medical assistance worldwide through International SOS.
- Cost savings: Amazon Travel & Events negotiates exclusive discounts with preferred suppliers. You access corporate rates on 400+ airlines, millions of hotel rooms, and car rental agencies that are not available on consumer booking sites.
- Booking simplicity: Access over 400 airlines and millions of hotel rooms through pre-vetted travel suppliers who meet rigorous safety and service standards.
- Sustainability: In line with Amazon's commitment to The Climate Pledge, purposeful travel options such as lower-emission rail and eco-conscious hotels are available. Select sustainable travel options when available.
- Service quality: Pre-screened travel providers meet high standards for reliability and customer care.

## **Before you book**

Review section 3.0 [General Booking Guidelines](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#4ec58a98-347a-e36a-54e0-278512f5781b) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy before booking.

title: Where Can I Locate My Previously Booked Trips? (Regional)
h1: Where Can I Locate My Previously Booked Trips? (Regional)
markdown:
# **Where can I locate my previously booked trips?**

Select Trips Library from the main menu in the [Self-Service Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) to access current and past trips, including invoices.

## **Archived or missing trips**

If a trip is no longer visible or has been archived, open a request through [Travel RIVER](https://river.amazon.com/?org=GTE) to access your historical booking records.

## P**rovider-managed bookings**

Bookings made directly through travel providers (including rail) must be managed directly with the provider.

title: Why is my booking out of policy?
h1: Why is my booking out of policy?
markdown:
# **Why is my booking out of policy?**

The "Out of Policy" flag in the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) helps you identify options that comply with the [Global Travel and Expense Policy](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#1). It does not prevent you from completing your booking.

## **What the Global Travel and Expense Policy considers**

- Lowest Logical Fare in economy with a seat assignment

- Lowest hotel rates

- Preferred car rates for the approved car type

## **Out of Policy vs. Policy Violation**

Out of Policy: Your selection exceeds policy guidelines but may be justified for business reasons

Yes — provide a reason for your selection.

Policy Violation: Your selection violates a hard rule (for example, booking first class). No — the booking cannot be completed.

## **When an out-of-policy booking is justified**

You may proceed with an out-of-policy booking when there is a valid business reason. Examples include:

- A hotel that is out of policy but closer to your business meetings

- An intermediate SUV during inclement weather for safety reasons

Provide a brief reason when prompted. This serves as an audit tool to ensure flexibility while maintaining safety and frugality.

title: What size rental car am I allowed to book?
h1: What size rental car am I allowed to book?
markdown:
# **What size rental car am I allowed to book?**

Book up to intermediate size vehicles (mini, economy, or compact) through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/).

Refer to section 6.1 Rental Car [Vehicle Size](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#6) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

## **Electric and hybrid vehicles**

Amazon encourages the use of electric or hybrid rental vehicles where available. If the only electric or hybrid vehicles available are standard or full-size, you may book these instead of an intermediate size vehicle.

## **Exceptions for larger vehicles**

Standard, full-size cars, intermediate SUVs, or vans are permitted in the following situations:

- Three (3) or more travelers traveling together and sharing the rental vehicle
- Large work equipment requiring additional cargo space
- Pre-approved medical reasons
- Safety exceptions (for example, AWD/4WD in winter in locations prone to blizzards)

## **Safe driving requirements**

Consider the risks associated with driving, especially outside your home country and after long flights.

### **Driver requirements**

- Hold a driving license suitable for the type of car rented and permitted by local law.
- Be familiar with local driving conditions before renting.

### **Risk management**

- Car rental is not advised unless you are familiar with local conditions.
- Follow [Travel Risk Management](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelRiskManagement/Home/) (TRM) ground transportation recommendations for elevated risk destinations in TRM advisories.

title: I need to change my travel plans (Global Access)
h1: I need to change my travel plans (Global Access)
markdown:
# **I need to change my travel plans**

Use the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) to cancel or rebook your trip. Self-service supports cancellations and modifications for most bookings.

Refer to section 3.10 [Itinerary Changes and Cancellations](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#3-10) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

## **To cancel a flight**

- Log in to the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/).
- Navigate to Trips > Upcoming and select your trip.
- Select Cancel next to the flight.
- Confirm the cancellation twice to complete.

## **To modify a flight**

- Select your segment and select Modify.
- Choose the flight to change.
- Update your details.
- Search for new flights and complete checkout.

Watch this quick video: [Modify Your Flight & Hotel](https://broadcast.amazon.com/videos/1594156).

title: What Am I Allowed to Book?
h1: What Am I Allowed to Book?
markdown:
# **What am I allowed to book?**

Travelers are strongly encouraged to book preferred suppliers identified in the [Self-Service Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/). Preferred supplier rates typically include flexible terms and conditions and additional benefits.

Refer to section 3.11 [Preferred Suppliers](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#c4c58a98-3505-2b70-3124-861fc6175249) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

## **Why book preferred suppliers? **

- Preferred suppliers are vetted for safety, security, and environmental considerations.
- Preferred rates include flexible terms and conditions.
- Loyalty program eligibility (not available in all countries or with all providers)
- Additional traveler benefits are included with preferred supplier bookings.

title: What Is the Process for Booking a Seat? (Global Access)
h1: What Is the Process for Booking a Seat? (Global Access)
markdown:
# **What is the process for booking a seat?**

Book the Lowest Logical Fare with a seat assignment and baggage included. The [Self-Service Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) highlights in-policy fares for easy selection.

Refer to section 4.7 [Air Travel Reimbursable Incidentals](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#76c58a98-34c4-136c-3e99-4d007e22b32c) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

## **EMEA and APAC travelers: ancillary purchases**

Fares that include a standard seat or baggage may sometimes exceed the Lowest Logical Fare limits in EMEA and APAC regions. If this occurs:

- Purchase the seat or baggage separately as an ancillary item directly with the airline.
- Expense the additional cost separately.
- Save all receipts for any ancillary purchases.

## **How to select a seat when included in your fare**

Seat maps are available during the shopping path only. Use them to compare seat availability across flights before making your selection.

### **During booking**

A seat may be auto-assigned when you select a flight at checkout. You may be able to select a different seat — view the seat map at checkout and choose your preferred seat before completing your booking.

### **After booking**

Change your seat once ticketed by:

- Navigating to Trips > My Trips in the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool
- Using the airline mobile app directly (varies by airline)

Watch this quick video: [How to Change Your Seat](https://broadcast.amazon.com/videos/1656533)

title: How Do I Request Medical Accommodation for Travel?
h1: How Do I Request Medical Accommodation for Travel?
markdown:
# **How do I request medical accommodation for travel?**

Contact [Disability and Leave Services](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/AET/Reyes-org/global-Travel/) (DLS) to request travel accommodations due to a physical or disability-related impairment or limitation.

Refer to section 3.5 [Travelers with Additional Needs](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#dac58a98-3508-3b21-6d99-2fb50bd5db01) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

## **Who authorizes travel accommodations?**

Only DLS can authorize travel accommodations. Amazon Travel & Events and your manager cannot authorize accommodation requests.

## **How to request**

Visit the [DLS site](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/AET/Reyes-org/global-Travel/) for information on the process and requirements. For Candidate or New Hires, contact the [Applicant Candidate Accommodation Team (ACAT)](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/AET/Reyes-org/DLS-Applicant-Candidate-Accommodation/).

title: How Do I Set Up My Travel Profile? (Japan)
h1: How Do I Set Up My Travel Profile? (Japan)
markdown:
# **How do I set up my Travel Profile?**

You must create a Travel Profile in the [Self-Service Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) before booking business travel. Your Travel Profile securely stores your personal information, passport details, payment method, and travel preferences.

Refer to section 3.4 [Traveler Profiles](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#52c58a98-3509-0d43-4f83-20fcd24c2fec) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

## **What your Travel Profile stores**

- Personal information: mobile phone number and date of birth (varies by country)

- Passport details (for international travel)

- Corporate or personal credit card (required to secure hotel reservations)

- Travel preferences and loyalty program numbers (optional)

## **Quick start videos**

[Travel Quick Start Videos (Japan)](https://broadcast.amazon.com/channels/1303/playlists/18326)

## **How to book after profile setup**

- Standard bookings: Log in to the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) and search for flights, hotels, car rentals, or rail options.
- Last-minute bookings (within 6 hours of departure): Contact a [Travel Counselor](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) during business hours. For same-day travel outside the 6-hour window, book online through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/).

title: Can I upgrade my fare using miles?
h1: Can I upgrade my fare using miles?
markdown:
# **Can I upgrade my fare using miles?**

Yes. After booking travel through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/), you may contact the airline directly to request an upgrade using your personal loyalty miles. Mileage upgrades cannot be processed through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) — you must request them directly with your airline.

Refer to section 3.12 [Loyalty Programs](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#c6c58a98-350b-aaa2-8c87-d9cb7f980da2) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

## **Important limitations**

- Airline policies vary: Not all airlines allow upgrades with miles for tickets purchased through third-party booking platforms.
- Fare class restrictions: Basic economy and deeply discounted fares are typically ineligible for mileage upgrades.
- Verification required: Review your specific fare rules and restrictions before requesting an upgrade.

title: How do I report a car accident while traveling for business?
h1: How do I report a car accident while traveling for business?
markdown:
# **How do I report a car accident while traveling for business?**

Contact emergency services first if there are injuries or immediate safety concerns. Then visit [Amazon Travel Risk Management](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelRiskManagement/Home/) for the damage reporting process. Once you have completed these steps, inform your manager.

Refer to section 6.8 [Car Accidents](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#62c58a98-3514-2159-75a6-abd568753d90) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

title: Do I need a visa for travel?
h1: Do I need a visa for travel?
markdown:
# **Do I need a visa for travel?**

You must complete a pre-travel immigration assessment through the International Business Travel Guided Experience before traveling internationally. This assessment determines your specific visa and immigration requirements based on your destination, citizenship, and trip details.

Refer to section 3.9 [Visas and Passports](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#3-9) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

## **What the assessment covers**

- Visa requirements for your destination country
- Immigration documentation needed based on your citizenship
- Trip-specific requirements based on duration and purpose of travel

title: Am I Allowed to Upgrade My Fare Class? (Global Access)
h1: Am I Allowed to Upgrade My Fare Class? (Global Access)
markdown:
# Am I Allowed to Upgrade My Fare Class? (Global Access)

Yes. Amazon reimburses only the Lowest Logical Fare in economy class. You must pay the difference between the policy-approved fare and the upgraded class out of pocket.

Refer to section 4.1 [Airline Cabin Class](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#4-1) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

## **How to upgrade **

- Search and select your preferred upgraded cabin class in the [Self-Service Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) (desktop only). The fare indicator displays the policy-approved amount and personal upgrade cost.
- On the Out of Policy pop-up, select Skip suggestions and proceed to the checkout page, then select Next.
- On the Checkout page, review the breakdown of the two amounts.
- Confirm your personal card is set as the form of payment.
- Acknowledge the booking contains a personal upgrade.
- Select Book flight to finalize your reservation.

You pay the full amount upfront and submit the policy-approved portion for reimbursement. The invoice/Itinerary you receive includes a Personal Upgrade Summary for your Expense Report.

Watch this quick video: [How to Book a Personal Upgrade](https://broadcast.amazon.com/videos/1687601).

## **Manager notification**

Your manager receives an email notification indicating you have added a personal upgrade. No manager action is required.

title: Am I allowed to upgrade my fare class? (Regional)
h1: Am I allowed to upgrade my fare class? (Regional)
markdown:
# **Am I allowed to upgrade my fare class?** (Regional)

Yes. Amazon reimburses only the Lowest Logical Fare in economy class. You must pay the difference between the policy-approved fare and the upgraded class out of pocket.

Refer to section 4.1[Airline Cabin Class](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#4-1) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

## **How to upgrade**

- Contact a Travel Counselor and request a personal upgrade for your trip.
- Pay for the entire ticket cost upfront using your own payment method.
- Obtain documentation showing the price difference between the Lowest Logical Fare and the upgraded class.
- When you expense your trip, claim reimbursement only for the documented Lowest Logical Fare noted by the travel services provider.

Upgrade fees and premium seating fees are not reimbursable.

## **Manager notification**

Your manager receives an email notification indicating you have added a personal upgrade. No manager action is required.

title: Can I get permission to upgrade for long-haul flights?
h1: Can I get permission to upgrade for long-haul flights?
markdown:
# **Can I get permission to upgrade for long-haul flights?**

Yes. Amazon has a defined process for granting cabin upgrade exceptions through the Premium Economy Accommodation (PEA) program. Travelers who meet specific criteria are approved to self-book premium economy for flights of 14 hours or more (excluding layovers).

## **Eligibility requirements**

To qualify for PEA, you must meet both criteria:

- Book all travel through Amazon Travel & Events.
- Complete four (4) or more one-way trips exceeding 14 hours (excluding layover time) in the last 12 months.

## **How PEA selection works**

No action is required from you. Once you fulfill the minimum requirements, your name is automatically added to the PEA list.

- Your [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) is automatically updated to allow booking premium economy flights.
- Qualifications are monitored daily.
- Once minimum criteria are fulfilled, you maintain status for one year from the qualifying date.
- No manual review or employee action is required.

## **Checking your PEA status**

Open a request through [Travel RIVER](https://river.amazon.com/?org=GTE&q0=8110c0c9-104e-4744-85d6-041cbc64db91) to verify your PEA status. Amazon Travel Support can verify status but cannot provide exceptions.

## **What PEA does not cover**

PEA exceptions do not apply to all flights. The exception applies only to flights of 14 hours or more (excluding layovers).

title: Can I Combine Work and Personal Travel?
h1: Can I Combine Work and Personal Travel?
markdown:
# **Can I combine work and personal travel?**

Yes. You can extend business trips to add personal time with line manager approval. Amazon reimburses only the costs associated with the business portion of your trip.

Refer to section 3.6 [Extended Trip for Personal Time](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#38c58a98-3502-4cf8-8a85-21cba63fea34) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

## **Requirements**

Obtain line manager approval before extending your trip.

Amazon reimburses only business-related costs.

Personal expenses incurred during the extension are your responsibility.

## **More information**

For details on reimbursement guidelines for combined trips, visit [Travel RIVER](https://river.amazon.com/?org=GTE) and the [Amazon Travel & Events Wiki](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/).

title: How Do I Set Up My Travel Profile? (Global Access)
h1: How Do I Set Up My Travel Profile? (Global Access)
markdown:
# **How do I set up my Travel Profile?**

You must create a Travel Profile in the [Self-Service Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) before booking business travel. Your Travel Profile securely stores your personal information, passport details, payment method, and travel preferences.

Refer to section 3.4 [Traveler Profiles](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#52c58a98-3509-0d43-4f83-20fcd24c2fec) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

## **What your Travel Profile stores**

- Personal information: mobile phone number and date of birth (varies by country)
- Passport details (for international travel)
- Corporate or personal credit card (required to secure hotel reservations)
- Travel preferences and loyalty program numbers (optional)

**Important: **Some information will be pre-populated for you—verify that your name exactly matches your government-issued ID.

## **Quick start guides**

[Travel Profile Quick Start Guide (Global Access) for Travelers](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/NewTravelSystem/)

[Travel Profile Quick Start Guide (Global Access) for Travel Arrangers](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Travel_Arranger_NewTravelsystem/)

## **How to book after profile setup**

- Standard bookings: Log in to the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) and search for flights, hotels, car rentals, or rail options.
- Last-minute bookings (within 6 hours of departure): Contact a [Travel Counselor](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) during business hours. For same-day travel outside the 6-hour window, book online through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/).

title: I need to change my travel plans (Regional)
h1: I need to change my travel plans (Regional)
markdown:
# **I need to change my travel plans**

Use the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) to cancel or rebook your trip. Self-service supports cancellations and modifications for most bookings.

Refer to section 3.10 [Itinerary Changes and Cancellations](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#3-10) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

## **How to cancel or modify your flight **

- Navigate to your Upcoming Trips in the Travel section of your home page.
- If online self-service changes are not available for your booking, contact a [Travel Counselor](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) for assistance.
- If further support is needed, visit the [Amazon Travel & Events Wiki](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) for more information.

title: Can I earn personal miles or loyalty points when traveling for business?
h1: Can I earn personal miles or loyalty points when traveling for business?
markdown:
# **Can I earn personal miles or loyalty points when traveling for business?**

Yes. You can earn personal loyalty miles and points when traveling for business through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/). This applies to airlines, hotels, and rental cars.

Refer to section 3.12 [Loyalty Programs](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#3-12) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

## **Eligibility restrictions**

Employees based in Sweden, Norway, Denmark, and Israel are not eligible to participate in supplier point programs due to payroll tax compliance requirements.

## **How to earn miles**

- Locate more information on the Amazon Travel & Events [Preferred Travel Providers and Promotions Wiki](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/BusinessTravelPromotions/).
- Sign up for loyalty programs.
- Add your loyalty program numbers to your Travel Profile.
- Miles and rewards are automatically credited to your personal account after each trip.

## **Important requirements**

- Ensure your loyalty numbers are accurate in your Travel Profile to avoid missing credits.
- Your Travel Profile name and your loyalty program name must match exactly.
- Contact the travel provider directly if miles are not credited after your trip.

title: Do I Need Rental Car Insurance for Work Travel?
h1: Do I Need Rental Car Insurance for Work Travel?
markdown:
# **Do I need rental car insurance for work travel?**

Amazon provides Collision Damage Waiver (CDW) and Liability Damage Waiver (LDW) insurance for business rentals when you book through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) and use preferred suppliers.

Refer to section 6.2 [Car Rental Insurance](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#88c58a98-3515-6808-3fb9-b6456aec9e1b) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

## **What is covered**

Collision Damage Waiver (CDW)

Liability Damage Waiver (LDW)

Deductibles may apply based on rental location

## **What to do at the rental counter**

Decline additional insurance at the rental counter unless legally required and not already included in your rate.

title: Do I Need Travel Insurance?
h1: Do I Need Travel Insurance?
markdown:
# **Do I need travel insurance?**

Amazon provides travel insurance and assistance coverage for business travel. You do not need to purchase separate travel insurance.

Refer to the section 10.4 [Travel Insurance/Loss or Damage](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#a4c58a98-34fe-cd45-17a3-7cdb4b9ec853) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

## **Your responsibilities while traveling**

Understand your coverage options before traveling.

Safeguard your luggage and personal belongings during business travel.

Follow proper procedures for reporting loss or damage.

## **Coverage details and claims**

For details on coverage, loss or damage to personal property guidelines, and how to file claims, visit the [Business Travel Insurance and Assistance Program](https://inside.hr.amazon.dev/content/inside/us/en/employment/your-career-global/insurance-and-assistance-program.html) page.

title: Where Can I Locate a Trail Code?
h1: Where Can I Locate a Trail Code?
markdown:
# **Where can I locate a Trail code?**

[Trail](https://trail.amazon.dev/trips) is Amazon's system that tracks business travel for employees based in the United States. It ensures you complete necessary tax documentation to avoid tax penalties for you and the company.

Refer to the [Tax Approval Process & Policy for International Travel](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/Tax_Approval_Process_&_Policy_for_International_Business_Travel/) page.

## **How to request a Trail code**

Request a code for your booking by visiting the [Trail](https://trail.amazon.dev/trips) workflow.

## **Who this applies to**

This applies only to U.S.-based employees.

title: Am I Allowed to Book Personal Travel in the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool?
h1: Am I Allowed to Book Personal Travel in the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool?
markdown:
# **Am I allowed to book personal travel in the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool?**

No. The [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) is for business travel only. Amazon's preferred rates for airlines are reserved for business travel and are not available for personal trips.

Refer to section 3.7 [Personal Travel](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#64c58a98-3504-2cb0-1873-9f43546b5385) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

## **Why personal travel is not permitted in the tool**

Amazon's preferred rates are negotiated exclusively for business travel.

Maintaining business-only usage protects negotiated rate agreements with suppliers.

## **Personal travel options**

For leisure travel promotions and personal booking options, visit [Amazon Extras](https://extrasforamazon.com/app/home).

title: Where do I locate Travel Counselor contact information?
h1: Where do I locate Travel Counselor contact information?
markdown:
# **Where do I locate Travel Counselor contact information?**

Find [Travel Counselor](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) contact information — including phone, email, chat, and hours by country — on the [Amazon Travel & Events Wiki](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/). Travel Counselors are available 24/7, 365 days per year in most countries.

Contact a [Travel Counselor](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) from the phone number listed in your Travel Profile. This ensures proper call routing and identification.

Chat is available within the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) in most countries. Chat does not support new reservations.

## **Before you call: Tips for faster service**

- Have your Trip ID ready — this helps Travel Counselors assist you more quickly
- Update your contact information — ensure your Travel Profile includes your current phone number for proper call routing
- Departing within 72 hours? — you are automatically prioritized and moved to the top of the call queue
- Do not want to wait on hold? — use the Callback Feature

## ** International calling**

Travelers outside their home country must have an international calling plan to reach the designated country contact number. If you do not have an international calling plan, use chat or email for assistance.

title: What Hotel Accommodations am I Allowed to Book?
h1: What Hotel Accommodations am I Allowed to Book?
markdown:
# **What hotel accommodations am I allowed to book?**

Book the lowest refundable standard hotel room rate on a single-occupancy basis near your work destination through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/). All business travel must be booked within Amazon Travel & Events.

Refer to section 5.0 [Accomodation](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#dec58a98-346b-920c-1662-8dbc2b8e05fb) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

## **Hotel location**

Book accommodations close to your work destination through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) or a [Travel Counselor](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/).

## **Hotel room type**

Amazon reimburses the lowest refundable standard hotel room rate on a single-occupancy basis.

## **Why book Amazon preferred hotels**

Amazon negotiates with leading hotels conveniently located near Amazon offices. Preferred hotels offer:

- Competitive rates
- Flexible cancellation policies
- Loyalty program eligibility (not available in all countries or with all providers)
- Safety and security vetting

While you may occasionally find a less expensive non-negotiated option, these rates may come with restrictions. Booking preferred rates contributes to overall cost savings across all travelers and provides greater flexibility if your plans change.

### **Found a cheaper rate outside the **[Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/)**?**

Contact a [Travel Counselor](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) to check if the same room/rate offer can be booked through Amazon Travel & Events. The offer must match the original room type and rate exactly.

## **Long-term stay accommodation (7+ nights)**

When staying in the same location for 7 or more nights, Corporate Serviced Apartments can offer additional space, comfort, amenities, and savings.

### **Unit types**

Select a studio apartment/flat or one-bedroom apartment/flat, whichever is less expensive.

### **Approval requirements**

Long-term stay accommodation (Corporate Serviced Apartments only) requires L8+ approval.

## **Shared economy properties**

The use of shared apartments or rental homes (including Airbnb) is strongly discouraged due to safety and security concerns. Use shared economy properties only when no suitable hotel or Corporate Serviced Apartment is available.

title: Why can I not see the Amazon form of payment when booking my flight?
h1: Why can I not see the Amazon form of payment when booking my flight?
markdown:
# **Why can I not see the Amazon form of payment when booking my flight?**

If you cannot select the Amazon form of payment (Amazon Central Bill Solution), your booking likely includes one or more personal or ancillary charges that are not eligible for central billing.

## **Common reasons the Amazon form of payment is unavailable**

- Personal upgrades (for example, premium cabin, extra legroom)
- Seat selection fees
- Baggage fees
- Other ancillary charges
- Personal travel segments

## **Why this matters**

The [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) does not support the Amazon form of payment for personal add-ons or ancillary services. You must pay these using an alternate payment method.

## **Additional consideration**

Some airlines require multi-factor authentication for payment processing. This also requires an alternate form of payment.

## **Need further assistance**

If you believe you should have access to the Amazon form of payment and none of these conditions apply, contact [Amazon Travel Support](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) for assistance.

title: Why am I receiving a new hotel confirmation I did not request?
h1: Why am I receiving a new hotel confirmation I did not request?
markdown:
# **Why am I receiving a new hotel confirmation I did not request?**

[Amazon Travel & Events](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/) uses automated auditing technology that monitors your hotel reservations after booking to find lower rates. When a better rate becomes available, the system automatically rebooks your hotel to capture savings.

## **What stays the same**

- Your hotel property
- Your check-in and check-out dates
- Your room type and preferences
- All other reservation details

## **What changes**

- Your confirmation number (you receive a new one)
- Your rate (it is lower)

## **Why this happens**

Hotel rates fluctuate due to demand, cancellations, and promotional pricing. The automated system monitors for price drops and secures savings automatically.

## **What you need to do**

- No action is needed from you. Rebooking happens automatically.
- Some exemptions may apply based on specific booking circumstances.
- Always use your new confirmation number when checking in or contacting the hotel.

title: What Flights Am I Allowed to Book?
h1: What Flights Am I Allowed to Book?
markdown:
# **What flights am I allowed to book?**

Book the Lowest Logical Fare in Coach/Economy Class that includes a seat assignment through the [Self-Service Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/).

Refer to section 4.1 [Airline Cabin Class](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#dec58a98-34c2-2f68-ea13-80ffad035827) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

## **Non-stop flight preference**

You may prioritize non-stop flights over connecting flights whenever possible and practical.

## **Exception for non-stop flights**

If a non-stop flight is not the Lowest Logical Fare, consult with your manager to determine the most appropriate flight option, balancing frugality and well-being.
